perturbation.original_text: (e) " Restricted Period " means the period commencing on the date of this Agreement and ending on the date which is five (5) years from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: (e) " Restricted Period " means the period commencing on the date of this Agreement and ending only if the Buyer continues to employ at least 50% of the Seller's former employees.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Restricted Period' is a fixed term of five years. The modified definition introduces a condition that makes the end date dependent on the Buyer's employment practices. This creates a contradiction with the overall structure of the agreement, particularly sections 2 and 4, which assume a fixed five-year period for non-competition and non-solicitation. The contradiction arises in how those sections would be enforced. For example, if the buyer drops below 50% threshold, does the Non-competition immediately become unenforceable?
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-competition. At all times from and after the date of this Agreement and until the expiration of the Restricted Period, Seller and Guarantor shall not: (a) directly or indirectly engage in, be employed by, own, manage, operate, provide financing to, control or participate in the ownership, management or control of, or otherwise have an interest (whether, subject to Section 5, as a stockholder, director, officer, employee, representative, subcontractor, partner, consultant, proprietor, agent or otherwise) in, or cause, authorize, aid or assist any other person to own, manage, operate, provide financing to, control or otherwise have an interest in, any business or any person who is engaged in any business that directly or indirectly competes or intends to compete with the Restricted Business anywhere in the world, unless Seller or Guarantor purchase or own less than five percent (5%) of capital stock in a publicly held company; or (b) directly, indirectly or otherwise by letters, circulars or advertisements, and whether for itself or on behalf of any other person, canvass or solicit or, directly or indirectly, cause or authorize to be solicited, or enter into or effect, or, directly or indirectly, cause or authorize to be entered into or effected, any business or orders for businesses competing with the Restricted Business from any person who (i) at the time of the Agreement or within two years prior to the date of the Agreement, has been, a customer or client, or (ii) is an active prospect to be a customer or client, in each case, of the Seller at the time of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Restricted Period' from a fixed five-year period to one that is contingent on the Buyer maintaining a certain level of employment. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text (Non-competition clause), which assumes a fixed Restricted Period. The non-competition clause states that the Seller cannot compete 'until the expiration of the Restricted Period'. If the Restricted Period's length is now dependent on the buyer's employment practices due to the change, this directly contradicts the assumption of a fixed period and renders the enforcement of the non-competition clause ambiguous, creating a strong in-text contradiction. If the buyer doesn't retain 50% of the seller's employees, the non-compete becomes void. This creates a structural flaw as per the definition.

perturbation.original_text: At all times from and after the date of this Agreement and until the expiration of the Restricted Period, Seller and Guarantor shall not: (a) directly or indirectly engage in, be employed by, own, manage, operate, provide financing to, control or participate in the ownership, management or control of, or otherwise have an interest (whether, subject to Section 5, as a stockholder, director, officer, employee, representative, subcontractor, partner, consultant, proprietor, agent or otherwise) in, or cause, authorize, aid or assist any other person to own, manage, operate, provide financing to, control or otherwise have an interest in, any business or any person who is engaged in any business that directly or indirectly competes or intends to compete with the Restricted Business anywhere in the world, unless Seller or Guarantor purchase or own less than five percent (5%) of capital stock in a publicly held company; or (b) directly, indirectly or otherwise by letters, circulars or advertisements, and whether for itself or on behalf of any other person, canvass or solicit or, directly or indirectly, cause or authorize to be solicited, or enter into or effect, or, directly or indirectly, cause or authorize to be entered into or effected, any business or orders for businesses competing with the Restricted Business from any person who (i) at the time of the Agreement or within two years prior to the date of the Agreement, has been, a customer or client, or (ii) is an active prospect to be a customer or client, in each case, of the Seller at the time of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: At all times from and after the date of this Agreement and until the expiration of the Restricted Period, Seller and Guarantor shall not: (a) directly or indirectly engage in, be employed by, own, manage, operate, provide financing to, control or participate in the ownership, management or control of, or otherwise have an interest (whether, subject to Section 5, as a stockholder, director, officer, employee, representative, subcontractor, partner, consultant, proprietor, agent or otherwise) in, or cause, authorize, aid or assist any other person to own, manage, operate, provide financing to, control or otherwise have an interest in, any business or any person who is engaged in any business that directly or indirectly competes or intends to compete with the Restricted Business anywhere in the world, unless Seller or Guarantor purchase or own less than five percent (5%) of capital stock in a publicly held company; or (b) directly, indirectly or otherwise by letters, circulars or advertisements, and whether for itself or on behalf of any other person, canvass or solicit or, directly or indirectly, cause or authorize to be solicited, or enter into or effect, or, directly or indirectly, cause or authorize to be entered into or effected, any business or orders for businesses competing with the Restricted Business from any person who (i) at the time of the Agreement or within two years prior to the date of the Agreement, has been, a customer or client, or (ii) is an active prospect to be a customer or client, in each case, of the Seller at the time of the Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller and Guarantor may engage in the Restricted Business if Buyer fails to pay the Purchase Price outlined in the Asset Purchase Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Section 2 outlines the non-competition agreement. By adding 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller and Guarantor may engage in the Restricted Business if Buyer fails to pay the Purchase Price outlined in the Asset Purchase Agreement', a direct contradiction is introduced. The first part of Section 2 prohibits competition, while this addition permits it under certain conditions. This creates uncertainty about the enforceability of the non-compete clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-competition. At all times from and after the date of this Agreement and until the expiration of the Restricted Period, Seller and Guarantor shall not: (a) directly or indirectly engage in, be employed by, own, manage, operate, provide financing to, control or participate in the ownership, management or control of, or otherwise have an interest (whether, subject to Section 5, as a stockholder, director, officer, employee, representative, subcontractor, partner, consultant, proprietor, agent or otherwise) in, or cause, authorize, aid or assist any other person to own, manage, operate, provide financing to, control or otherwise have an interest in, any business or any person who is engaged in any business that directly or indirectly competes or intends to compete with the Restricted Business anywhere in the world, unless Seller or Guarantor purchase or own less than five percent (5%) of capital stock in a publicly held company; or (b) directly, indirectly or otherwise by letters, circulars or advertisements, and whether for itself or on behalf of any other person, canvass or solicit or, directly or indirectly, cause or authorize to be solicited, or enter into or effect, or, directly or indirectly, cause or authorize to be entered into or effected, any business or orders for businesses competing with the Restricted Business from any person who (i) at the time of the Agreement or within two years prior to the date of the Agreement, has been, a customer or client, or (ii) is an active prospect to be a customer or client, in each case, of the Seller at the time of the Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller and Guarantor may engage in the Restricted Business if Buyer fails to pay the Purchase Price outlined in the Asset Purchase Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction to the original non-compete agreement. The original text strictly prohibits competition by the Seller and Guarantor. The addition, 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller and Guarantor may engage in the Restricted Business if Buyer fails to pay the Purchase Price outlined in the Asset Purchase Agreement,' explicitly allows them to compete under specific circumstances, directly conflicting with the initial prohibition. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text which makes the contradiction evident. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Seller and Guarantor recognize that all such Confidential Information is and shall remain the sole property of the Buyer, free of any rights of Seller or Guarantor, and acknowledges that the Buyer and its Affiliates have a vested interest in assuring that all such Confidential Information remains secret and confidential. Therefore, the Seller and Guarantor agree that at all times from after the date hereof, they will not, directly or indirectly, without the prior written consent of the Buyer, disclose to any person, firm, company or other entity (other than the Buyer or any of its Affiliates) any Confidential Information, except to the extent that (i) any such Confidential Information becomes generally available to the public or trade, other than as a result of a breach by the Seller or Guarantor of this Section 3, or (ii) any such Confidential Information becomes available to the Seller or Guarantor on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Seller, Guarantor, Buyer or any of their Affiliates or advisors; provided, that such source is not known by the Seller or Guarantor to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other obligation of secrecy to, the Seller, Guarantor, Buyer or another party.
perturbation.changed_text: Seller and Guarantor recognize that all such Confidential Information is and shall remain the sole property of the Buyer, free of any rights of Seller or Guarantor, and acknowledges that the Buyer and its Affiliates have a vested interest in assuring that all such Confidential Information remains secret and confidential. Therefore, the Seller and Guarantor agree that at all times from after the date hereof, they will not, directly or indirectly, without the prior written consent of the Buyer, disclose to any person, firm, company or other entity (other than the Buyer or any of its Affiliates) any Confidential Information, except to the extent that (i) any such Confidential Information becomes generally available to the public or trade, other than as a result of a breach by the Seller or Guarantor of this Section 3, or (ii) any such Confidential Information becomes available to the Seller or Guarantor on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Seller, Guarantor, Buyer or any of their Affiliates or advisors; provided, that such source is not known by the Seller or Guarantor to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other obligation of secrecy to, the Seller, Guarantor, Buyer or another party. Notwithstanding the above, Seller and Guarantor are permitted to disclose Confidential Information to potential investors in Seller.
perturbation.explanation: Section 3 outlines the non-disclosure of confidential information. By adding 'Notwithstanding the above, Seller and Guarantor are permitted to disclose Confidential Information to potential investors in Seller', a direct contradiction is introduced. The first part of Section 3 prohibits disclosure, while this addition permits it under certain circumstances. This creates uncertainty about the scope of confidentiality and the enforceability of the non-disclosure clause, since disclosure to potential investors might violate the intent of the original non-disclosure agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. Seller and Guarantor acknowledge that it is the policy of the Buyer to maintain as secret and confidential all Confidential Information, and the parties hereto recognize that Seller and Guarantor have acquired Confidential Information. Seller and Guarantor recognize that all such Confidential Information is and shall remain the sole property of the Buyer, free of any rights of Seller or Guarantor, and acknowledges that the Buyer and its Affiliates have a vested interest in assuring that all such Confidential Information remains secret and confidential. Therefore, the Seller and Guarantor agree that at all times from after the date hereof, they will not, directly or indirectly, without the prior written consent of the Buyer, disclose to any person, firm, company or other entity (other than the Buyer or any of its Affiliates) any Confidential Information, except to the extent that (i) any such Confidential Information becomes generally available to the public or trade, other than as a result of a breach by the Seller or Guarantor of this Section 3, or (ii) any such Confidential Information becomes available to the Seller or Guarantor on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Seller, Guarantor, Buyer or any of their Affiliates or advisors; provided, that such source is not known by the Seller or Guarantor to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other obligation of secrecy to, the Seller, Guarantor, Buyer or another party. Notwithstanding the above, Seller and Guarantor are permitted to disclose Confidential Information to potential investors in Seller.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 3 establishes a strict non-disclosure policy regarding Confidential Information. The changed text adds a 'Notwithstanding the above' clause, explicitly permitting disclosure to potential investors. This directly contradicts the initial non-disclosure agreement, creating a clear and significant inconsistency as disclosure is both prohibited and permitted. The contradicted text, which is the whole section, becomes ambiguous regarding the confidentiality of the information.

perturbation.original_text: At all times from and after the date of this Agreement and until the expiration of the Restricted Period, Seller and Guarantor shall not, directly, indirectly or otherwise by letters, circulars or advertisements, and whether for themselves or on behalf of any other person: (a) solicit or, directly or indirectly, cause to be solicited for employment, any persons who (i) are, at the time of solicitation of employment, employees of the Seller, Buyer or any of their respective Affiliates, or (ii) are, at the time of solicitation of employment, sales representatives or employees thereof, retained by the Buyer or any of its Affiliates; or (b) employ or, directly or indirectly, cause to be employed, any persons who (i) are, at the time of such action, employees of the Buyer or any of its Affiliates, or (ii) are, at the time of such action, sales representatives or employees thereof, retained by the Buyer or any of its Affiliates; provided, however, that this Section 4 shall not prohibit Seller or Guarantor from employing or soliciting the employment any person who (A) is an employee of Seller as of the Agreement Date and (B) is not offered employment by Buyer as of the Agreement Date.
perturbation.changed_text: At all times from and after the date of this Agreement and until the expiration of the Restricted Period, Seller and Guarantor shall not, directly, indirectly or otherwise by letters, circulars or advertisements, and whether for themselves or on behalf of any other person: (a) solicit or, directly or indirectly, cause to be solicited for employment, any persons who (i) are, at the time of solicitation of employment, employees of the Seller, Buyer or any of their respective Affiliates, or (ii) are, at the time of solicitation of employment, sales representatives or employees thereof, retained by the Buyer or any of its Affiliates; or (b) employ or, directly or indirectly, cause to be employed, any persons who (i) are, at the time of such action, employees of the Buyer or any of its Affiliates, or (ii) are, at the time of such action, sales representatives or employees thereof, retained by the Buyer or any of its Affiliates; provided, however, that this Section 4 shall not prohibit Seller or Guarantor from employing or soliciting the employment any person who (A) is an employee of Seller as of the Agreement Date and (B) is not offered employment by Buyer as of the Agreement Date. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller and Guarantor are permitted to solicit and employ Buyer's employees if those employees were previously employed by the Seller.
perturbation.explanation: Section 4 outlines the non-solicitation agreement. The addition of 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller and Guarantor are permitted to solicit and employ Buyer's employees if those employees were previously employed by the Seller' creates a direct contradiction. The first part of Section 4 prohibits solicitation and employment of Buyer's employees, while this addition permits it under certain conditions. This introduces ambiguity and potential disputes about the enforceability of the non-solicitation clause, as it becomes unclear when and which of Buyer's employees are protected under this clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Solicitation. At all times from and after the date of this Agreement and until the expiration of the Restricted Period, Seller and Guarantor shall not, directly, indirectly or otherwise by letters, circulars or advertisements, and whether for themselves or on behalf of any other person: (a) solicit or, directly or indirectly, cause to be solicited for employment, any persons who (i) are, at the time of solicitation of employment, employees of the Seller, Buyer or any of their respective Affiliates, or (ii) are, at the time of solicitation of employment, sales representatives or employees thereof, retained by the Buyer or any of its Affiliates; or (b) employ or, directly or indirectly, cause to be employed, any persons who (i) are, at the time of such action, employees of the Buyer or any of its Affiliates, or (ii) are, at the time of such action, sales representatives or employees thereof, retained by the Buyer or any of its Affiliates; provided, however, that this Section 4 shall not prohibit Seller or Guarantor from employing or soliciting the employment any person who (A) is an employee of Seller as of the Agreement Date and (B) is not offered employment by Buyer as of the Agreement Date. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller and Guarantor are permitted to solicit and employ Buyer's employees if those employees were previously employed by the Seller.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a 'Notwithstanding the foregoing' clause that directly contradicts the original non-solicitation agreement. The original text and contradicted text strictly prohibit the solicitation and employment of Buyer's employees, while the added clause permits it under certain conditions (if those employees were previously employed by the Seller). This creates a clear and direct conflict within Section 4, making it uncertain which employees are protected and introducing ambiguity regarding the enforceability of the non-solicitation clause. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
